steps.title: Set up the difference and factor
steps.content:
The time difference is $31 \times 269 - 31 \times 236$. Factor out 31:
$$31(269 - 236)$$
Calculate the difference in orbital periods:
$$269 - 236 = 33$$

steps.title: Multiply by the number of orbits
steps.content:
Compute the total difference:
$$31 \times 33 = 1023$$
The second moon takes 1023 more days to orbit the planet 31 times.
